etymology.description: phonetic 來 (lái, originally representing 'wheat') became corrupted to 木 (mù, 'tree') + semantic 子 (zǐ, 'child') -> 李
etymology.interpretation: Originally a phono-semantic compound with 來 (lái, originally 'wheat') providing sound and 子 (zǐ, 'child') lending meaning. Over time, 來 was corrupted to 木 (mù, 'tree'), suggesting a 'plum tree', which is also associated with the surname Li, well-known in Chinese culture.

examples.chinese: 李老师是我们的数学老师(李老師是我們的數學老師)。
examples.pinyin: Lǐ lǎoshī shì wǒmen de shùxué lǎoshī.
examples.english: Mr. Li is our math teacher.

examples.chinese: 李先生邀请我们去他的茶馆(李先生邀請我們去他的茶館)。
examples.pinyin: Lǐ xiānshēng yāoqǐng wǒmen qù tā de cháguǎn.
examples.english: Mr. Li invited us to his tea house.

examples.chinese: 我常常和李小姐去公园散步(我常常和李小姐去公園散步)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ chángcháng hé Lǐ xiǎojiě qù gōngyuán sànbù.
examples.english: I often go for walks in the park with Miss Li.
